title: What to know about Achillea millefolium before using it medicinally
intro_desc: Achillea millefolium, commonly known as yarrow, is a medicinal herb that helps alleviate symptoms of menstrual cramps, digestive issues, and inflammation associated with arthritis. Its main health benefits include reducing fever, promoting wound healing, and supporting cardiovascular health. The herb's therapeutic properties include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic effects. Bioactive compounds in yarrow include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and volatile oils. People often make yarrow tea by infusing the dried leaves and flowers in hot water to utilize its medicinal properties.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Journal of ethnopharmacology, an ethanolic extract of Zingiber officinale rhizomes was found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory properties that reduced carrageenan-induced paw swelling in rats. Additionally, the extract exhibited antipyretic activity by reducing yeast-induced fever in rats and showed antimicrobial properties by significantly inhibiting the growth of both Gram-positive and Gram-negative bacteria. Furthermore, the extract demonstrated hypoglycaemic activity by lowering blood glucose levels in rabbits, indicating its potential in managing certain health conditions.
uses_description: The main medicinal uses of Achillea millefolium are the relief of menstrual cramps due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, primarily attributed to sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. This plant also has antispasmodic properties that aid in the relief of digestive issues, such as cramps and spasms, resulting from its essential oil, which contains camphor and borneol. The antipyretic properties of Achillea millefolium, particularly its sesquiterpene lactones, contribute to its ability to reduce fever. Furthermore, its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, including the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, facilitate the wound healing process and alleviate inflammation. Additionally, the plant's essential oil, which contains camphor and borneol, has been used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis, and infections, like those caused by Staphylococcus aureus, as well as to soothe insect bites and wounds, and relieve rheumatism.

uses_list.description: Its essential oils and flavonoids have been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with menstrual cramps.
The plant's roots and leaves contain bitter compounds that can stimulate digestive enzymes and improve digestion, making it a traditional remedy for digestive issues such as bloating and indigestion.
Its antipyretic properties have been reported to help reduce fever by inducing sweating and promoting the elimination of toxins from the body.
The plant's flavonoids and terpenoids have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and alleviate conditions such as arthritis and eczema.
Its essential oils and flavonoids have been shown to have antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which can help to prevent infection and promote wound healing.
The plant's anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties have been reported to help alleviate the pain and stiffness associated with rheumatism.
Its antiseptic properties have been traditionally used to clean and disinfect wounds, reducing the risk of infection and promoting healing.
The plant's essential oils have been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which can help to reduce itching and swelling associated with insect bites.
Its essential oils have been traditionally used to help relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma due to their expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties.
The plant's essential oils and flavonoids have antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which can help to prevent and treat infections such as urinary tract infections and fungal infections.

uses_modern_western_medicine_description: In modern western medicine, Achillea millefolium is used to treat menstrual cramps, thanks to its anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, which also contribute to its effectiveness in wound healing by promoting tissue repair and reducing bacterial growth. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory compounds have been shown to combat inflammation, a condition often associated with various health issues, including digestive problems caused by impaired gut motility. Furthermore, its antimicrobial properties make it a valuable treatment for infections, including those caused by bacteria, viruses, and fungi, and its anxiolytic effects have been linked to reduced anxiety in patients. Achillea millefolium is also used to alleviate the symptoms of insect bites, as well as gastrointestinal issues such as bloating and gas, and it has been used to treat rheumatism by reducing joint inflammation and pain. The plant's anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties also make it a potential treatment for skin conditions, including acne and eczema.
uses_traditional_chinese_medicine_descri: In traditional Chinese medicine, Achillea millefolium is used to treat fever, with its antipyretic properties helping to reduce body temperature and alleviate discomfort. It is also used to address digestive issues, such as indigestion and bloating, through its carminative and anti-inflammatory effects. Furthermore, Achillea millefolium is valued for its anti-inflammatory properties, which make it an effective treatment for inflammation, rheumatism, and menstrual cramps. The plant is also used to alleviate menstrual issues, including irregular periods and heavy bleeding, due to its ability to regulate hormonal balance and reduce uterine contractions. Additionally, Achillea millefolium is used to treat a range of other conditions, including diarrhea, respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, insect bites, and menstrual pain.
uses_ayurvedic_medicine_description: In ayurvedic medicine, Achillea millefolium is used to treat a variety of health conditions, including digestive issues, which are alleviated by its antispasmodic properties. Achillea millefolium is also employed to alleviate menstrual cramps, caused by its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. Additionally, it is used to reduce fever, which is a common symptom of various infections, including those caused by Achillea millefolium's antibacterial properties. Furthermore, it is utilized to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis, as well as skin conditions, like eczema, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Its antiseptic properties also make it useful for treating wounds, promoting wound healing, reducing fever, preventing infections, and alleviating the discomfort caused by insect bites.
uses_unani_medicine_description: In unani medicine, Achillea millefolium is used to treat various health conditions. It is often employed to reduce fever, while its antiseptic and antibacterial properties make it effective in treating wounds. The plant's anti-inflammatory properties also alleviate symptoms of rheumatism and joint pain. Additionally, Achillea millefolium is used to address digestive issues such as irritable bowel syndrome and gastritis, as well as menstrual issues like dysmenorrhea and menorrhagia. Furthermore, it is also used to treat skin infections, menstrual disorders, and inflammation, promoting wound healing and alleviating digestive problems.
uses_homeopathic_medicine_description: In homeopathic medicine, Achillea millefolium is used to treat menstrual cramps by relieving uterine spasms, its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties also aid in alleviating digestive issues such as irritable bowel syndrome and indigestion. Additionally, the plant's anti-inflammatory properties make it effective in treating fever, reducing body temperature and alleviating discomfort. Achillea millefolium is also used to treat insect bites, its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties helping to soothe and calm the affected area, reducing swelling and pain. Furthermore, its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties are useful in treating inflammation, migraines, and rheumatism, providing relief from pain and discomfort. The plant's antiseptic and antifungal properties also make it effective in treating wounds, anxiety, and headaches, promoting healing and reducing stress.
benefits_description: Achillea millefolium reduces inflammation due to its sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids content, which inhibit pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines. This plant relieves menstrual cramps by blocking prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause uterine contractions, and flavonoids, which have estrogenic and anti-estrogenic effects. Reducing anxiety is also a benefit, as Achillea millefolium contains apigenin, a flavonoid that binds to GABA receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and sedation. Improving digestion is facilitated by the plant's volatile oils, which stimulate digestive enzymes and relax the smooth muscles of the gastrointestinal tract. The antimicrobial, antifungal, and antiviral properties of Achillea millefolium's essential oils, including camphor and borneol, allow it to treat skin issues, soothe muscle pain, fight bacterial infections, lower blood pressure, boost the immune system, and combat cancer cells by inhibiting the growth of cancer cells and inducing apoptosis.

benefits_list.description: This benefit is due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids in this plant, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate conditions such as arthritis and other inflammatory diseases.

This plant's ability to relieve menstrual cramps and other symptoms associated with PMS is due to the presence of flavonoids, particularly kaempferol and quercetin, which have a uterotonic effect, meaning they help to tone and strengthen the uterus, and also have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties.

This plant's anxiolytic properties are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have a sedative effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation.

This plant's ability to improve digestion is due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, helping to soothe digestive issues such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other gastrointestinal disorders.

This plant's ability to treat skin issues such as eczema, acne, and other skin conditions is due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties, helping to soothe and heal the skin.

This plant's ability to soothe muscle pain is due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, helping to reduce muscle spasms and alleviate pain associated with conditions such as fibromyalgia and arthritis.

This plant's ability to fight bacterial infections is due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have antimicrobial properties, helping to inhibit the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms.

This plant's ability to lower blood pressure is due to the presence of flavonoids, particularly quercetin and kaempferol, which have vasodilatory properties, helping to relax blood vessels and reduce blood pressure.

This plant's ability to boost the immune system is due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have immunomodulatory properties, helping to stimulate the production of white blood cells and enhance the body's natural defense against infections.

This plant's ability to combat cancer cells is due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have anticancer properties, helping to inhibit the growth and spread of cancer cells and induce apoptosis, or programmed cell death.

benefits_circulatory_system_description: Achillea millefolium improves blood flow due to its vasodilatory properties, which stimulate the widening of blood vessels, allowing for greater oxygen and nutrient delivery. Enhancing circulation is another benefit of the plant, as its saponins and flavonoids help to increase the efficiency of blood flow, reducing the risk of circulatory disorders. Reducing inflammation is also essential for maintaining healthy blood vessels, and Achillea millefolium's anti-inflammatory properties, particularly its sesquiterpene lactones, contribute to this effect by suppressing inflammatory responses. Stimulating vasodilation, decreasing blood pressure, relaxing veins, fighting blood clots, boosting oxygenation, lowering cholesterol, and protecting heart health are all interconnected benefits of the plant's ability to increase blood flow and reduce inflammation, ultimately contributing to a healthier circulatory system. The plant's antioxidant and antimicrobial properties further support its cardioprotective effects by preventing oxidative stress and infections that can compromise heart health.
benefits_digestive_system_description: Achillea millefolium reduces inflammation, primarily due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and neutralize free radicals. The plant alleviates bloating through its carminative and antispasmodic properties, which relax the smooth muscles in the digestive tract and facilitate the release of trapped gas. Achillea millefolium also relieves nausea with its sedative and anti-emetic properties, which calm the central nervous system and reduce vomiting. Soothing diarrhea is another benefit of the plant, attributed to its demulcent and antimicrobial properties, which protect the mucous membranes and prevent the growth of pathogenic bacteria. The plant calms colitis and eases constipation with its anti-inflammatory and laxative properties, respectively, while it also heals ulcers, relaxes irritation, prevents spasms, and treats gastritis due to its antimicrobial, antioxidant, and anti-ulcer properties, which reduce oxidative stress and promote tissue repair.
benefits_endocrine_system_description: Achillea millefolium regulates hormones by exerting estrogenic and anti-estrogenic effects, balancing the endocrine system. This plant reduces inflammation, which is responsible for many endocrine disorders, thereby creating a favorable environment for hormone regulation. Achillea millefolium stabilizes metabolism by modulating the expression of genes involved in glucose and lipid metabolism. The plant balances thyroid function by influencing the expression of thyroid hormones and the enzymes involved in their production, and soothes the adrenal glands by reducing cortisol levels. Additionally, Achillea millefolium supports reproductive health, calms ovarian function, heals pancreatic tissue, boosts immunity, and stimulates the pituitary gland to produce gonadotropins, all through its antimicrobial, antioxidant, and anti-inflammatory properties.
benefits_integumentary_system_descriptio: Achillea millefolium treats acne due to its antimicrobial properties, which inhibit the growth of Propionibacterium acnes bacteria, a primary cause of acne. The plant reduces inflammation by containing anti-inflammatory compounds such as sesquiterpene lactones, which suppress the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines. Soothing irritations in the integumentary system is also a benefit, thanks to its antioxidant properties that neutralize free radicals and protect against oxidative stress. Eczema is cured by Achillea millefolium due to its anti-inflammatory and immunosuppressive properties, which reduce inflammation and modulate the immune response. The plant prevents rashes, heals burns, calms itches, tones skin, and cleans wounds by exerting its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, promoting a healthy integumentary system and wound healing process.
benefits_lymphatic_system_description: Achillea millefolium stimulates lymph flow by exerting a gentle diuretic effect, which helps to eliminate excess fluids and reduce lymph swelling. It supports lymph drainage by breaking down and dispersing lymph congestion, while also enhancing lymph function through its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and promote healing. Additionally, Achillea millefolium boosts lymphatic health by acting as an antioxidant, neutralizing free radicals that can damage lymph tissue and compromise immune function. Furthermore, it improves lymphatic filtration by increasing lymph fluid circulation, which helps to remove toxins and waste products from the body. By promoting lymph tissue regeneration and enhancing immune system response, Achillea millefolium also increases lymph node size, further supporting the lymphatic system's ability to defend against infection and disease.
benefits_musculoskeletal_system_descript: Achillea millefolium relaxes muscles due to its antispasmodic properties, which help to reduce muscle spasms and cramping. This plant eases joint pain by exerting anti-inflammatory effects, reducing swelling and pain associated with joint conditions. The anti-inflammatory properties of Achillea millefolium also reduce inflammation, which is a major contributor to various musculoskeletal conditions. Additionally, Achillea millefolium soothes arthritis, calms tendinitis, relieves bursitis, reduces swelling, sooths fibromyalgia, and eases tendonitis due to its anti-inflammatory, anti-arthritic, and analgesic properties. The analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties of Achillea millefolium also calm menstrual pain, making it a valuable herbal remedy for managing various musculoskeletal and gynecological conditions.
benefits_nervous_system_description: Achillea millefolium reduces anxiety by exerting a sedative effect on the nervous system, attributed to its flavonoid and sesquiterpene content, which possess a calming influence on the central nervous system. This plant relieves stress through its antioxidant properties, which neutralize free radicals and protect neurons from oxidative damage, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation. Achillea millefolium improves focus due to its essential oils, which stimulate the release of neurotransmitters responsible for attention and concentration, such as acetylcholine and dopamine. Boosting energy is another benefit of Achillea millefolium, as its flavonoids and sesquiterpenes increase blood flow and oxygenation to the brain, enhancing mental clarity and alertness. The plant enhances mood through its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce inflammation in the brain and promote the release of neurotransmitters associated with feelings of happiness and well-being, such as serotonin and endorphins, which in turn regulates sleep, calms nerves, improves memory, relaxes muscles, and stabilizes temperament.
benefits_reproductive_system_description: Achillea millefolium regulates menstruation by exerting a sedative effect on the uterine muscles, while antimicrobial properties prevent infections that can disrupt menstrual cycles. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties in Achillea millefolium relax uterine muscles, thereby reducing the risk of miscarriage and improving fertility by creating a more favorable environment for implantation. Stimulating ovulation is also facilitated by the estrogenic activity present in the plant, which relieves menstrual pain by releasing tension in the uterine muscles. Additionally, Achillea millefolium reduces endometriosis by inhibiting the growth of endometrial tissue and supports pregnancy by maintaining a healthy uterine environment, which in turn eases menopausal symptoms by alleviating hormonal imbalances and enhances reproductive health by promoting a balanced hormonal profile.
benefits_respiratory_system_description: Achillea millefolium reduces inflammation by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, which alleviate the discomfort associated with respiratory issues. It relieves congestion due to its expectorant properties, helping to clear mucus from the airways. Eases coughing is another benefit of Achillea millefolium, attributed to its demulcent properties, soothing the throat and reducing irritation. Decreases mucus production is facilitated by its antitussive properties, while alleviates wheezing is a result of its bronchodilatory effects, allowing for easier breathing. Treats asthma, fights infections, soothes coughs, cures bronchitis, and prevents allergies are all health benefits of Achillea millefolium, largely due to its antimicrobial, antioxidant, and antispasmodic properties, which work together to provide comprehensive respiratory support.
benefits_urinary_system_description: Achillea millefolium prevents kidney damage due to its antioxidant properties that neutralize oxidative stress and its anti-inflammatory properties that reduce inflammation in the kidneys. It reduces urinary tract infections through its antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of pathogenic microorganisms. Treats urinary incontinence by toning urinary muscles with its antispasmodic properties, reducing muscle spasms and improving bladder control. Relieves bladder irritation through its anti-inflammatory properties that reduce inflammation in the bladder and its antioxidant properties that protect it from oxidative damage, which also decreases urine frequency by regulating the frequency of bladder contractions. Eases menstrual cramps, supports prostate health, reduces bladder spasms, and prevents enlarged prostate through its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which also relieve the body of free radicals that can cause these conditions.
properties_desc: The medicinal properties of Achillea millefolium are multifaceted. Its antimicrobial properties are attributed to the presence of essential oils, particularly camphor and borneol, which exhibit inhibitory effects against various pathogens. The antioxidant properties of the plant are due to the flavonoids and phenolic acids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, which scavenge free radicals and protect against oxidative stress. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties of Achillea millefolium are mediated by the sesquiterpenes and labdane diterpenes, which reduce inflammation and relax smooth muscles, respectively. Furthermore, the analgesic, pain-relief, antiseptic, antiviral, diuretic, and expectorant properties of the plant are attributed to its bioactive compounds, including volatile oils, saponins, and phenolic acids, which exhibit analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities.

properties_list.description: Achillea millefolium has been found to exhibit antimicrobial properties, specifically against certain bacteria and fungi, due to the presence of compounds like sesquiterpenes and flavonoids which inhibit the growth of microorganisms.

Achillea millefolium possesses antioxidant properties, attributed to the presence of flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and phenolic acids which can scavenge free radicals and protect against oxidative stress.

The plant has been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, attributed to the presence of flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and phenolic acids which can reduce inflammation and inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Achillea millefolium has been found to exhibit analgesic properties, specifically due to the presence of sesquiterpenes and flavonoids which can reduce pain by inhibiting the production of pain-causing chemicals in the body.
The plant has been traditionally used for pain relief, and its analgesic properties have been confirmed by scientific studies, making it a potential natural remedy for managing pain.
Achillea millefolium has been found to exhibit antispasmodic properties, attributed to the presence of flavonoids and sesquiterpenes which can relax muscles and reduce spasms.
The plant has been traditionally used as an antiseptic to prevent infection and promote wound healing, likely due to the presence of sesquiterpenes and flavonoids which have antimicrobial properties.
Achillea millefolium has been found to exhibit antiviral properties, specifically against certain viruses, due to the presence of flavonoids and sesquiterpenes which can inhibit viral replication.

The plant has been traditionally used as a diuretic to increase urine production and help manage conditions like hypertension and fluid retention, likely due to the presence of sesquiterpenes and flavonoids which can increase urine output.

Achillea millefolium has been traditionally used as an expectorant to help manage respiratory conditions like bronchitis and asthma, likely due to the presence of flavonoids and sesquiterpenes which can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs.

constituents.description: A type of plant compound, Flavonoids are characterized by their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.

A group of compounds found in Achillea millefolium, characterized by their foaming properties, bitter taste, and ability to form soap-like substances in water, with potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects.
Coumarins are a type of medicinal constituent found in Achillea millefolium, which possess anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and anticoagulant properties.
Phenolic acids from Achillea millefolium exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.
Apigenin is a flavone with anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and estrogen receptor binding properties.
Luteolin is a flavonoid that has antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.
Tannins from Achillea millefolium are astringent, antiseptic, and have antimicrobial properties.
Achillea millefolium contains Triterpenoids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Rosmarinic acid is a polyphenolic compound with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.
Achillea millefolium essential oils are rich in camphor, borneol, and bornyl acetate, which exhibit antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties.

constituents_desc: The main medicinal constituents of Achillea millefolium are flavonoids such as apigenin and luteolin which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, saponins which have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects, coumarins like scopoletin that possess anticoagulant and anti-inflammatory activities, and phenolic acids including caffeic acid and ferulic acid which display antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. The plant also contains sesquiterpene lactones which contribute to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The combination of these bioactive compounds makes Achillea millefolium a valuable source of natural remedies for various health issues. The antioxidant capacity of the plant is attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids which help in scavenging free radicals and protecting against oxidative stress. The medicinal properties of Achillea millefolium are primarily due to the synergy between its various bioactive constituents.
parts_desc: The most used parts of Achillea millefolium for medicinal purposes are flowers and leaves. The flowers of Achillea millefolium contain primarily flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and triterpenoids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, contributing to their traditional use in treating wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The leaves of Achillea millefolium are rich in sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and volatile oils, which possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic properties, making them useful in treating gastrointestinal issues, fever, and menstrual disorders. Both parts also contain essential oils with antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive compounds present in these parts are responsible for their medicinal properties, which have been utilized in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.

parts_list.description: The flowers of this plant are rich in flavonoids, particularly apigenin and luteolin, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, providing relief from allergies, promoting wound healing, and protecting against infections.

The leaves of this plant are rich in sesquiterpene lactones, primarily achillol and dihydroachillol, which possess anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antiseptic properties, relieving pain, reducing inflammation, and protecting against infections.

preparations.description: Tincture made from this plant is used to treat digestive issues, menstrual cramps, and anxiety, and to improve wound healing due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties.
preparations.overview: Achillea millefolium tincture is a herbal preparation used to treat menstrual cramps, inflammation, digestive issues, fever, wound healing, skin conditions, wounds, respiratory issues, and rheumatism. It's one of the most commonly used herbal remedies due to its effectiveness. The most common effect of this preparation is its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, making it a popular choice for treating various health issues. Achillea millefolium tincture is typically made with the flowers and leaves of the plant, which are rich in essential oils and compounds that provide its medicinal properties. The process of making this preparation is moderately difficult, requiring knowledge of herbal extraction methods and equipment.
recipe: Gather fresh Achillea millefolium flowers and leaves, and clean them thoroughly.
Combine 1 part of the cleaned flowers and leaves with 2 parts of 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the mixture daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Label the tincture and store it in a cool, dark place, taking 20-30 drops (about 1-2 mL) as needed.

preparations.description: Salve made from this plant is used topically to treat inflammation, wounds, and minor skin irritations, and to relieve pain and itching associated with insect bites and minor burns.
preparations.overview: Achillea millefolium salve is a common herbal preparation used to treat a variety of issues, including bruises, insect bites, wounds, sprains, skin irritation, burns, minor cuts, eczema and menstrual cramps. This preparation is widely used due to its effectiveness in wound healing and reducing pain. One of the common effects of using Achillea millefolium salve is the reduction of inflammation and the promotion of tissue repair. The salve is typically made from the flowers and leaves of the Achillea millefolium plant, which contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. However, making Achillea millefolium salve can be challenging due to the need for precise measurement and preparation of the plant materials, as well as the requirement for careful sterilization and preservation techniques to prevent spoilage.
recipe: Combine 2 cups of fresh Achillea millefolium flowers and leaves in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in 4 cups of carrier oil, such as olive or jojoba oil, for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Add 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of vitamin E oil to the infused oil, and heat the mixture in a double boiler until the beeswax is fully melted.
Pour the salve mixture into small tin containers and let it cool completely before use.

preparations.description: Tea made from this plant is used to treat fever, reduce inflammation, relieve menstrual cramps, and improve digestion, as well as to calm anxiety and insomnia.
preparations.overview: Achillea millefolium tea is a preparation used to treat menstrual cramps, digestive issues, inflammation, anxiety relief, fever, cold symptoms, skin conditions, stress, wound healing and rheumatism. This preparation is very commonly used, especially in folk medicine. One of its most common effects is reducing inflammation and relieving pain. It's made by infusing flowers and leaves of the Achillea millefolium plant in hot water. This preparation is easy to make at home by steeping the dried flowers and leaves in boiling water for 5-10 minutes, then straining and serving.
recipe: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Achillea millefolium flowers and leaves.
Steep the herbs in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup or teapot.
Add honey to taste, if desired, and stir well.
Drink 1/2 to 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day, as needed.

preparations.description: Essential oil made from this plant is used topically to treat pain, reduce inflammation, and improve wound healing, and internally as an antispasmodic and to treat digestive issues, such as bloating and cramps.
preparations.overview: Achillea millefolium essential oil is used to treat menstrual cramps, insect bites, digestive issues, inflammation, wound healing, skin irritation, rheumatism, wounds, fever and pain relief. It is a common preparation in herbal medicine, widely used due to its effectiveness. One common effect of this oil is its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and ease pain. The preparation is made from the flowers, leaves and roots of the Achillea millefolium plant, which are typically harvested and distilled to extract the essential oil. The process of making this oil is complex and requires great skill, as it involves precise temperature control and equipment to ensure the oil is extracted safely and effectively.
recipe: Gather 100 grams of fresh Achillea millefolium flowers, leaves, and roots in equal proportions.
Dry the harvested plant material in a well-ventilated area for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Use a steam distillation apparatus to extract the essential oil from the dried plant material, with a ratio of 1:5 plant material to distilled water.
Collect the distilled essential oil, filtering it through a coffee filter to remove any impurities, resulting in approximately 10-15 mL of Achillea millefolium essential oil.
Dilute the essential oil with a carrier oil such as jojoba or sweet almond oil at a 2% dosage (2 drops of essential oil per 1 teaspoon of carrier oil) to achieve the desired strength and stability.

preparations.description: Suppository made from this plant is used for soothing and protecting irritated rectal mucosa, treating hemorrhoids, and relieving anal inflammation and itching.
preparations.overview: Achillea millefolium suppository is a herbal preparation used to treat various conditions, including endometriosis, menorrhagia, menstrual cramps, dysmenorrhea, cystitis, vaginitis, hemorrhoids, gastroenteritis, vaginal infections, and inflammation. It is relatively uncommon as a treatment option, likely due to its limited availability and the preference for conventional medications. The suppository has a unique effect on the body, which is not well understood, but it is thought to be related to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The preparation is typically made from a combination of leaves, roots, and flowers of the Achillea millefolium plant, which requires a high degree of skill and expertise to harvest and process correctly. This can make the preparation difficult to produce and standardize, contributing to its relative rarity.
recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of dried Achillea millefolium leaves, roots, and flowers, then grind them into a fine powder.
Combine the powdered herbs with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax and coconut oil are fully incorporated.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool until it reaches a consistency suitable for molding, then pour it into suppository molds.
Insert one suppository, approximately 1-2 grams in weight, rectally 1-2 times a day as needed, under the guidance of a healthcare professional.

preparations.description: Decoction made from this plant is used to treat fever, rheumatism, digestive issues, and menstrual disorders, as well as to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing.
preparations.overview: Achillea millefolium decoction is a herbal preparation used to treat menstrual cramps, digestive issues, wound healing, fever, insect bites, inflammation, rheumatism, skin conditions, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal issues. It's a common preparation in traditional medicine, widely used in various cultures. A common effect of this decoction is its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to reduce pain and promote healing. The decoction is made with the leaves, flowers, and roots of the Achillea millefolium plant, which are combined and boiled in water to release their active compounds. Moderately difficult to make, the preparation requires careful measurement of the plant material and attention to the boiling time to ensure the optimal extraction of its active ingredients.
recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried leaves and flowers, 1 tablespoon of dried roots, and 4 cups of water.
Combine the dried leaves, flowers, and roots in a saucepan and pour in the water.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat and simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Drink 1/2 cup of the decoction 2-3 times a day as needed, or as directed by a healthcare professional.

preparations.description: Capsule made from this plant is used to treat menstrual cramps, inflammation, and digestive issues, and to improve wound healing and immune function.
preparations.overview: Achillea millefolium capsule is a herbal preparation used to treat menstrual cramps, inflammation, digestive issues, wound healing, rheumatism, fever, skin conditions, anxiety, antiseptic wounds, and stress relief. It is a common preparation in traditional medicine, widely used by practitioners and patients. Its common effect is to reduce inflammation and pain, making it a popular choice for various health conditions. The capsule is typically made from the leaves and flowers of the Achillea millefolium plant, which are rich in essential oils and flavonoids. However, making this preparation can be challenging due to the difficulty of extracting the active compounds from the plant material, requiring specialized equipment and techniques.
recipe: Gather 500g of fresh Achillea millefolium leaves and flowers from a clean and pesticide-free area.
Dry the collected leaves and flowers in a well-ventilated area or use a food dehydrator at 40°C for 2-3 hours.
Grind the dried leaves and flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice grinder.
Fill gelatin capsules with 250-500mg of the powdered Achillea millefolium and seal them properly.
Store the herbal capsules in an airtight container in a cool, dark place and take 1-2 capsules daily as needed, following a recommended dosage of 250-500mg per day.

preparations.description: Ointment made from this plant is used to treat minor wounds, skin irritations, and insect bites, and to reduce inflammation and relieve pain.
preparations.overview: Achillea millefolium ointment is used to treat wounds, insect bites, bruises, sprains, burns, eczema, skin irritations, rashes, and cuts. It's a common preparation used worldwide. Its effect is anti-inflammatory and antiseptic. This ointment is made from the flowers, leaves, and roots of the Achillea millefolium plant. The process of making Achillea millefolium ointment is complex and requires precise proportions of the plant's components to achieve the desired medicinal properties.
recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh Achillea millefolium flowers, leaves, and roots, and dry them in a cool, dark place.
Combine 1/2 cup of dried flowers, 1/4 cup of dried leaves, and 1/4 cup of dried roots in a clean glass jar.
Steep the dried plant material in 2 cups of carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Mix 1/4 cup of the herbal oil with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and heat the mixture in a double boiler until the beeswax melts.

preparation_desc: The most used herbal preparation of Achillea millefolium for medicinal purposes are tinctures, which contain concentrated plant extracts and are often used to treat digestive issues and menstrual cramps. Salves, typically applied topically, are used to soothe skin irritations and wounds, taking advantage of the plant's anti-inflammatory properties. Tea is another popular preparation, consumed as a drink to alleviate menstrual symptoms and promote relaxation. Essential oil, derived from the plant's flowers and leaves, is used in aromatherapy to ease anxiety and promote sleep, while suppositories are used to treat vaginal infections and hemorrhoids. A decoction is used to treat colds and respiratory issues, and capsules and ointments are also used to deliver the plant's medicinal properties to the body, providing targeted relief from pain and inflammation.

side_effects.side_effect_description: Skin, respiratory, or cardiovascular symptoms occur due to sensitivity to Achillea millefolium compounds.
High doses cause liver and kidney damage, and gastrointestinal symptoms.
Achillea millefolium increases the risk of bleeding when combined with anticoagulant medications.
Achillea millefolium contains sesquiterpene lactones and volatile oils that cause nephrotoxicity and potential kidney damage.
Achillea millefolium may enhance the effects of anticoagulant medications and increase bleeding risk, and may also interact with blood thinners, diabetes medications, and blood pressure medications.
Achillea millefolium consumption during pregnancy may cause premature uterine contractions and miscarriage.
Consuming large amounts of Achillea millefolium during pregnancy causes birth defects due to thujone toxicity, a teratogenic effect
Inhibition of lactation occurs due to estrogenic activity of the plant's compounds.
Ingestion causes nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, and abdominal pain.
Achillea millefolium essential oil inhalation can induce bronchospasm and exacerbate asthma symptoms due to its volatile oils, primarily borneol and camphor.

side_effects_overview: The possible side effects of improperly using Achillea millefolium are allergic reaction occurs due to its volatile oils, such as borneol and bornyl acetate, which can cause skin irritation and respiratory distress. Toxicity develops if ingested in large quantities, as it contains sesquiterpene lactones that can disrupt cellular processes. Bleeding risk increases due to its effect on platelet aggregation and clotting, as it may interfere with blood thinners like warfarin. Kidney damage happens over time if the plant's flavonoids and phenolic acids accumulate in the kidneys, causing oxidative stress and inflammation. If taken in conjunction with certain medications, interactions with medications occur, such as increased levels of digoxin and decreased efficacy of antacids. Pregnancy complications arise due to its uterotonic properties, which can stimulate uterine contractions and induce premature labor. Birth defects occur when the plant's teratogenic compounds interfere with embryonic development. Breastfeeding issues arise as its flavonoids and phenolic acids can pass into breast milk and affect the infant's digestive system. Gastrointestinal problems develop due to its emetic properties, which can cause nausea and vomiting. Respiratory issues occur when its volatile oils trigger asthma-like symptoms and exacerbate respiratory conditions like chronic obstructive pulmonary disease.

precautions.precaution_description: Achillea millefolium may interact with anticoagulant medications, consult a healthcare professional before use.
Accurately identify Achillea millefolium to distinguish it from similar-looking plants that may cause adverse reactions or interactions.
Achillea millefolium may stimulate uterine contractions and affect fetal development, so it is recommended to avoid its use during pregnancy.
Achillea millefolium contains sesquiterpene lactones, which are excreted in breast milk and potentially cause harm to the infant.
Excessive consumption of Achillea millefolium causes gastrointestinal upset and diarrhea.
Prolonged use of Achillea millefolium may cause allergic reactions, photosensitivity, and interactions with warfarin, as well as exacerbate epilepsy and other seizure disorders.
Achillea millefolium interacts with anticoagulant medications, such as warfarin, and increases the risk of bleeding.
Achillea millefolium toxicity increases with excessive consumption.

precautions_overview: The precautions to take before using Achillea millefolium medicinally are to consult a healthcare professional to ensure safe and effective use, determine the correct identification of the plant to avoid confusion with lookalike species, as its use in pregnancy is contraindicated due to potential uterine stimulation, and similarly, its use in breastfeeding is cautioned against due to limited safety data. The herb should be used in moderation, especially when consuming it for extended periods, as excessive consumption may lead to side effects. Monitoring for side effects such as gastrointestinal upset and allergic reactions is crucial when using Achillea millefolium, and users should be aware of potential interactions with medications, particularly those affecting blood clotting and liver function. To minimize risks, users should follow proper dosage guidelines to avoid accidental overdosing. Proper identification also ensures that the correct part of the plant, typically the flowers, is used for medicinal purposes.
identification: Achillea millefolium is a perennial herbaceous plant in the sunflower family (Asteraceae), characterized by its fern-like foliage composed of finely divided, pinnate leaves and flat-topped clusters of small, yellow or white flowers. It typically grows 2-4 feet tall, with a spread of 1-2 feet, and has a taproot system allowing it to thrive in well-drained soils. Native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, this species has been naturalized in many regions worldwide, including North America, Australia, and New Zealand, where it often grows in open areas, along roadsides, and in meadows. It has been cultivated for its ornamental value and is commonly found in gardens and along borders. Achillea millefolium is a hardy, adaptable plant that tolerates a range of temperatures and moisture levels, making it a popular choice for landscaping and conservation projects.
cultivation: Achillea millefolium is a hardy perennial that exhibits a clump-forming growth habit with a spreading tendency, requiring full sun to partial shade and well-drained soil with a slightly alkaline to neutral pH (6.0-7.0) to thrive. It performs best in temperatures between 15-25°C (59-77°F) and can tolerate drought but will benefit from regular watering to maintain optimal growth. To promote healthy growth, divide and replant Achillea millefolium every 3-4 years in the spring, and remove spent flowers to encourage new blooms. Additionally, avoid over-fertilizing, as this can lead to weak and leggy growth. Regular deadheading and staking may be necessary to prevent the plant's tall stems from flopping over, especially in windy or wet conditions.
history: Achillea millefolium is a plant with a long history of medicinal use, dating back to ancient civilizations such as the Greeks and Romans, who valued it for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. The plant's leaves, flowers, and roots have been used to treat various ailments, including wounds, bruises, and sprains, as well as menstrual cramps, fever, and digestive issues. In traditional European medicine, Achillea millefolium was used to calm nervous disorders and as an antiseptic to prevent infection in minor cuts and scrapes. The plant's antispasmodic properties have also been used to treat menstrual cramps and other gastrointestinal issues. In modern herbal medicine, Achillea millefolium is still used to treat similar conditions, with studies confirming its efficacy as an anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic agent.
